text.title: EF Core Data Layer Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the EF Core data-layer methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every entity MUST have a dedicated `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>` class under `Data/Configurations/`. `OnModelCreating` MUST call `ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly(...)` and contain no inline `builder.Entity<T>().Property(...)` calls.
rule.rationale: Inline configuration in `OnModelCreating` grows unbounded and hides entity-specific mapping detail behind one giant method; per-entity classes keep diffs scoped.

rule.statement: Every read-only query path MUST call `AsNoTracking()` (or use a projection that bypasses the change tracker). Repositories MUST NOT return `IQueryable<T>` — materialise with `ToListAsync`/`ToArrayAsync`/`FirstOrDefaultAsync` before crossing the layer boundary.
rule.rationale: Change tracker bloats memory on read paths; leaked `IQueryable` lets callers execute queries against a disposed context.

rule.statement: Every aggregate root MUST carry an optimistic-concurrency token: `[Timestamp] byte[] RowVersion` (SqlServer) or `[ConcurrencyCheck]` `xmin uint` (Postgres). Concurrent updates without a token are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a token, EF's last-write-wins silently corrupts data under concurrent edits.

rule.statement: Bulk updates and deletes MUST use `ExecuteUpdateAsync`/`ExecuteDeleteAsync` (EF Core 7+) — not load + mutate + SaveChanges loops.
rule.rationale: Load-mutate-save round-trips a row at a time; `ExecuteUpdateAsync` runs one SQL statement and never touches the change tracker.

rule.statement: Cross-cutting audit fields (`CreatedAt`, `UpdatedAt`, `UpdatedBy`) MUST be populated by a `SaveChangesInterceptor` registered once on the `DbContext`. Per-entity configuration MUST NOT set these in every class.
rule.rationale: Per-entity duplication drifts; one interceptor enforces a single canonical implementation across the model.

rule.statement: Migrations applied to shared dev or prod databases MUST be reviewed as SQL via `dotnet ef migrations script` BEFORE `dotnet ef database update`. Destructive operations (`DROP COLUMN`, `DROP TABLE`) require an explicit human checkpoint.
rule.rationale: Code-level diff hides destructive SQL (e.g. cascade FK reshuffle); SQL review surfaces the actual schema delta.
